data.title: Ramaco Resources started at Sell by Goldman Sachs on Brook mine skepticism
data.description:
Ramaco Resources (METC) -11.1% in Wednesday's trading as Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Sell rating and $16 price target, saying the company still needs to meaningfully de-risk its Brook Mine asset to warrant its current valuation.

Goldman's Brian Lee believes Ramaco's (METC) Brook Mine will be more expensive from an operating and capital standpoint than the company currently forecasts and sees insufficient demand for scandium to support the large volumes the company aims to produce.

Ramaco (METC) has been focused mostly on the production of met coal but its stock has dislocated from historical valuations due to a greater focus on its rare earth deposit, but Lee noted that the grades of the Brook Mine deposit are much lower than traditional assets; as a result, the analyst thinks further derisking is required to increase confidence in the commercial viability of the asset and sees meaningful risks of developing the Brook Mine asset.

While the Brook Mine aims to produce several minerals, scandium is expected to support 59% of the revenue despite only accounting for 5% of overall production, and the scandium market is already oversupplied, which has caused pricing to meaningfully decline over recent years, and the future demand outlook remains mostly limited to solid oxide fuel cells, Lee said.

The analyst also pointed to deteriorating market dynamics for met coal, driven by shifting supply-demand dynamics and heightened volatility, with market prices continuing to decline since the Q1 2022 peak, and he expects continued near-term weakness before seeing a gradual
improvement through 2028.

data.title: Trump jokes about firing Bessent if rates not lowered
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump, growing impatient with the Federal Reserve's pace of interest-rate cuts, quipped that he would oust Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent if he fails to help deliver lower interest rates.

“The only thing Scott’s blowing it on is the Fed,” Trump said at a U.S.-Saudi investment event in Washington. He added in a joking tone: “rates are too high, Scott, and if you don’t get it fixed fast, I’m going to fire" you. "Okay?”

Trump repeated that he would like to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell. On Tuesday, Trump said he has started interviewing candidates for the next Fed chief and hinted he has already settled on his choice. Powell's term ends in May 2026.

As Treasury head, Bessent has no authority to set rates. That responsibility lies with the central bank's Federal Open Market Committee. The FOMC has cut rates twice this year, though Trump has been pushing for far deeper and faster reductions. Trump has said he wants Bessent to lead the monetary authority, but Bessent doesn't want to leave his Treasury post.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Axalta shares seen rangebound as RBC downgrades stock after $25B Akzo Nobel merger deal
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets on Wednesday downgraded Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) to Sector Perform from a previous investment rating of Outperform, saying the company’s newly announced $25 billion all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) (OTCQX:AKZOY) offers limited near-term upside for shareholders.

The merger of equals, unveiled November 18, will create a coatings giant with roughly $17 billion in pro-forma revenue and $3.3 billion in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, supported by an estimated $600 million in cost synergies. Axalta (AXTA) investors will receive 0.6539 Akzo shares for each Axalta share, implying a takeout value of about $30.58, only a roughly 9% premium to Axalta’s pre-announcement share price, according to RBC.

RBC analysts led by Arun Viswanathan said the small premium, combined with muted growth trends across key coatings markets, tempers expectations for a meaningful rerating. The firm noted that most of the merger’s benefits, including synergy capture, tax advantages and potential index inclusion tied to a future NYSE-only listing, will take up to five years to fully materialize.

The combined company plans to operate across seven segments, including Decorative, Refinish, Industrial and Aerospace, and will target net leverage of 2.0 to 2.5 times. Both firms will suspend share repurchases until the transaction closes, expected in late 2026 or early 2027.

RBC cut its price target on Axalta (AXTA) to $30 from $35, citing slower structural growth in refinish and industrial coatings, a lack of competing bidders, and the long timeline before value is realized.

data.title: Exact Sciences likely needs to see over $100/share in takeover - analyst
data.description:
Exact Sciences (EXAS) will likely need to see over $100 a share in a takeover, according to  a William Blair analyst, after an earlier report that Abbott Labs (ABT) is talks to buy the cancer test maker.

Shares of Exact Sciences (EXAS) soared 21% to ~$84/share after Bloomberg reported the takeover discussions with a transaction possible within the coming days. Abbott Labs fell 3.6%.

"From a valuation perspective, we believe a takeout multiple between 6 and 7 times forward sales would be required," William Blair analyst Andrew Brackmann wrote in a note on Wednesday. "This would be a 35%-plus premium to the current multiple of just under 5 times, a roughly 20% premium to the five-year average of roughly 5.6 times, and in line with smaller but similarly growing profitable labs that trade in this range."

Exact Sciences (EXAS) is known for its Cologuard colon cancer test. 

"Overall, with limited details, EXAS would offer a solid brand and strong beachhead into the broader oncology testing market across screening," Barclays analyst Luke Sergott wrote in a note on Wednesday.

data.title: Bitcoin resumes slide after brief bounce, falling back to below $90K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) resumed its price decline Wednesday as the crypto struggled to hold the key $90K technical support level.

The highest-profile token retreated 4.5% in the last 24 hours to $88.8K as of 2:51 p.m. ET, reversing Tuesday's bounce to as high as $93.7K. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was off 30% from its all-time high of over $126K at press time.

"Bitcoin continues to work through a challenging market phase defined by weakening market structure, retreating speculative demand, and a decisive shift toward risk-off positioning across derivatives," Glassnode said in a report. "Spot-based demand remains absent, ETF flows are negative, and futures markets show no appetite to add leverage into weakness. Meanwhile, implied volatility, skew, and hedging flows all point toward heightened concern for near-term downside risk, with investors paying increasingly high premiums for protection."

Much of the cryptoverse was under pressure intraday. Ether (ETH-USD) dropped 7.9% to $2.87K, Monero (XMR-USD) -11%, Cronos (CRO-USD) -7.7%, Sui (SUI-USD) -9.6%, Uniswap (UNI-USD) -8.8%, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -8.6%, XRP (XRP-USD) -8.2% and Solana (SOL-USD) -6.9%.

Crypto-linked stocks were also trading in the red as major equity indices looked for direction. Strategy (MSTR), which recently disclosed it bought the BTC dip, tumbled 11%, Coinbase Global (COIN) -5%, Gemini Space Station (GEMI) -8.2%, Bullish (BLSH) -6.3%, Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) -7.6%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -8.2% and Circle Internet Group (CRCL) -11%.

The path forward for bitcoin (BTC-USD) "depends on whether demand can re-emerge around key cost-basis levels or whether current fragility gives way to a deeper corrective phase or bear market," as the market searches for stability, Glassnode added.

data.title: Nomura turns bullish on Sony citing improved growth prospects for entertainment unit
data.description:
• Nomura on Wednesday upgraded Sony (SONY) to "buy" from a previous investment rating of "neutral."
  • The research firm sees improved growth prospects for Sony's entertainment unit while continuing to expect consistently strong profits at its game & network services and music segments.
  • "The company has been working to expand subscription streaming businesses for some time, and we raise our rating to Buy because we think profit growth prospects have improved as these efforts start to bear fruit," Nomura said in its research note.
  • The research firm said it expects profit contributions to be driven by, among other things, PlayStation Plus subscriber growth, faster growth in music streaming, and improvements in the product mix for image sensors.
  • For the music segment, they said Sony will benefit from the renewal of contracts with digital service providers.
  • For the networks segment, Nomura noted Sony's y-o-y growth in subscriber numbers and a shift towards high-priced services.
  • "With rival Xbox Game Pass scheduled to hike prices sharply in November 2025, we expect PS Plus membership to remain on an upward trajectory and margins to remain high," Nomura said.

data.title: Qualcomm establishes AI engineering center in Riyadh with Humain
data.description:
Qualcomm Technologies (QCOM) is establishing an artificial intelligence engineering center with Humain, a Saudi Arabian-backed AI firm, in Riyadh, with plans to open the center as soon as next month.

It was one of several collaborations surfacing from the U.S.-Saudi AI investment forum today in Washington, D.C.

The new Qualcomm AI Engineering Center in Riyadh will support the rollout of 200 megawatts of data center capacity by Humain on Qualcomm solutions in 2026. The data centers will feature Qualcomm's cloud hardware and software, including the AI200 and AI250 rack solutions.

"Co-located with Humain, it will serve as a key technology and ecosystem development hub designed to streamline and accelerate deployment of the most advanced services based on Qualcomm AI solutions for data centers," said Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon.

The data center capacity buildout stems from a memorandum of understanding signed by Qualcomm and Humain in May 2025.

data.title: Stocks bounce off session lows and trade mixed after earlier market rally
data.description:
Wall Street looks for direction following an early rally that pushed the Nasdaq Composite up 1.5% on Wednesday. Markets are now navigating the fallout from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics canceling the delayed October jobs report while also awaiting earnings from AI powerhouse Nvidia (NVDA).

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) last traded higher by 0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was near even and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) tracked down in the red by 0.3%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, three of the 11 S&P segments were trading in positive territory with the Communication Services space leading the way. At the other end of the spectrum, Energy has suffered the most with oil (CL1:COM) down by 2.2%.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced Wednesday that it is canceling the release of the delayed October jobs report, citing an inability to collect survey data amid the ongoing government shutdown.

Treasury yields also inched up as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved higher by 1 basis point to 4.13% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up by 1 basis point to 3.58%. 

The Fed minutes were released and they highlighted the widening divide among its members over whether the policymakers need to lower rates further or not. And it doesn't look like the disagreement will resolve by the December meeting.

After two days of risk-off selling on AI valuation and spending concerns, investors will likely continue to be cautious until Nvidia (NVDA) reports after the bell. 

Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlighted that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak — its sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. 

"Nvidia is the holy grail on which the market bases its mood – such a concentrated focus on one sector ((AI)) has practically never been seen before in this form. Valuations are hot, as are expectations. In short: caution, yes – panic, no," Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker, said on X. 

Elsewhere on the earnings front, Target (TGT) was -3.6% after the retailer reported a decline in quarterly sales. Conversely, Lowe's (LOW) was +4.6% after the home improvement retailer raised its annual sales guidance. 

Additionally, the publication of delayed economic data after the government shutdown continued on Wednesday. The trade deficit in U.S. goods and services narrowed more than expected in August.

data.title: Star Bulk signals $204M in newbuilding financing plans while expanding Kamsarmax fleet into 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Star Bulk Carriers Corp. (SBLK) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Petros Pappas reported net income of $18.5 million and adjusted net income of $32.4 million, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $87 million for the third quarter. The company repurchased 250,000 shares for $4.4 million during the quarter and an additional 360,000 shares for $6.7 million since the start of Q4. "Our Board of Directors decided to continue prioritizing returns to shareholders given the company's strong position, declaring a dividend per share of $0.11 for the quarter payable on or December 18, 2025." (Co-Chief Financial Officer Christos Begleris)
  • Total cash was reported at $454 million, total debt at $1.028 billion, and undrawn revolver facilities provided additional liquidity of $115 million, resulting in pro forma liquidity of more than $570 million. The company has $91 million remaining from its renewed share repurchase program and currently holds 15 debt-free vessels with a market value of $336 million.
  • Time charter equivalent rate for the quarter was $16,634 per vessel per day, with combined daily OpEx and net cash G&A expenses at $6,421 per vessel per day.
  • Chief Operating Officer Nicos Rescos updated that operating expenses for Q3 2025 were $5,096 per vessel per day and net cash G&A expenses were $1,325 per vessel per day. He noted, “During October, we entered into 3 prompt recent renovation agreements with Hengli Shipbuilding for three 82,000 deadweight scrubber-fitted Kamsarmax newbuildings scheduled for delivery in Q3 2026.”
  • The company has secured $130 million in debt for five Qingdao newbuilding Kamsarmax vessels, with another $74 million expected for the three Hengli Kamsarmax vessels. Vessel sales during Q3 amounted to $75.5 million, with an additional $25 million generated in October. The fleet now totals 145 vessels on a fully delivered basis with an average age of 11.9 years.

Outlook

  • Head of Market Research Constantinos Simantiras stated, “Total dry bulk trade during 2025 is projected to expand by 1.4% in ton miles. During 2026, dry bulk demand is projected to increase by 2.1% in ton miles.”
  • The company expects grain trade to expand by 2% during 2025 and by 5.3% in 2026, and iron ore trade to expand by 0.8% in 2025 and 2.8% in 2026. Minor bulk trade is expected to expand by 5% during 2025 and by 2.1% in 2026.
  • The IMF forecast for global GDP growth is 3.1% for 2026, with Chinese GDP projected to slow to 4.2%.

Financial Results

  • Star Bulk reported positive cash flow from operating activities of $92 million for the quarter, resulting in an end-of-quarter cash balance of $457 million. The company declared a dividend of $0.11 per share and continued its share buyback program.
  • The time charter equivalent rate improved to $16,634 per day from $13,624 in the previous quarter. Combined daily OpEx and cash G&A per vessel increased slightly to $6,421 from $6,277.
  • Share repurchases for the quarter totaled $4.4 million, down from $54 million in the previous quarter. The company increased the number of debt-free vessels and improved the aggregate market value of these assets.

Q&A

  • Christopher Robertson, Deutsche Bank: Asked about expectations around the total net change in debt in 2026 considering $204 million in newbuilding financings. Christos Begleris clarified that $130 million has been secured for the first five vessels, with discussions ongoing for the remaining three. Final figures will be disclosed in March. On amortization, Begleris stated, “Our amortization will remain around the $50 million mark per quarter. What is happening is that some older facilities are getting refinanced. And then the new facilities for the new buildings have an amortization profile of 17 years, has not impacting in any major way the amortization profile of Star Bulk.”
  • Robertson also asked about dividend policy calculation. President Hamish Norton responded, “There has been no change to that. And we're so far above that level in terms of our cash balance that we -- it's not been an obstacle to any dividend payments in the last 2 years.”
  • Robertson inquired about strong rate performance in sub-cape segments. CEO Pappas explained, “There is a spillover effect from the bigger vessels. But let's not forget that grain trade improved by 11% during Q3 and that coal did very well as well during the third quarter.”
  • Omar Nokta, Jefferies: Asked about the rationale behind focusing on Kamsarmax newbuildings. CEO Pappas detailed, “We ordered Kamsarmaxes because our existing Kamsarmax fleet is getting older. So we need to do some renewal on that level. Second, we actually -- our S&P department managed to get very early deliveries during 2026, which we expect to be a good year. The prices were low. The vessels had scrubbers, so they're eco vessels. So we're happy with how they are doing, how they will be doing.”
  • Nokta followed up on Cape vessel strategy. CEO Pappas noted that Capes are expensive and hard to acquire: “Everybody keeps the Capes close to his chest and they are expensive and everybody whoever sells Capes likes to sell the worst performers that they have.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on financing, fleet strategy, and dividend policy, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with a focus on operational and capital allocation discipline.
  • Management maintained a confident and straightforward tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly emphasizing the company's ample liquidity and strong positioning. CEO Pappas and President Norton both stressed that dividend payments and capital needs are well covered.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, analyst tone was less focused on market volatility and more on capital structure and forward strategy, while management maintained a similar level of confidence, shifting focus toward growth and asset renewal.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company improved its time charter equivalent and daily vessel rates, increased its cash position, and reduced debt. Share buybacks slowed compared to Q2, but the dividend per share more than doubled from $0.05 to $0.11.
  • Strategic focus shifted from post-merger synergy realization and cost savings (Q2) to fleet renewal and capital allocation (Q3), with increased attention to newbuild financing and asset optimization.
  • Analysts shifted from questions about asset disposals and merger benefits in Q2 to queries about future debt structure and strategic asset class allocation in Q3.
  • Management’s tone remained confident, but the emphasis moved from integration and cost control in Q2 to liquidity, fleet renewal, and capital deployment in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted that debt amortization will remain around $50 million to $52 million per quarter for 2026, with refinancing of older facilities and long-term profiles for newbuild financings.
  • The company faces challenges in acquiring Capesize vessels due to high prices and limited availability, as well as ongoing market uncertainties driven by global trade dynamics and regulatory developments.
  • Management continues to dispose of older, less efficient vessels and invest in newbuilds to mitigate fleet aging and efficiency risks.

Final Takeaway

Star Bulk Carriers emphasized its strong liquidity, disciplined capital allocation, and proactive fleet renewal strategy as it prepares for 2026, backed by newbuilding financings and a robust balance sheet. Management underscored a commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks, while maintaining operational flexibility and targeting growth in the Kamsarmax segment. The company remains focused on navigating market dynamics and regulatory changes to sustain long-term value for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tech Voices: Summers leaves OpenAI, Warner-Udio, AMD-Cisco-Humain JV
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Former Treasury Secretary Larry Summers has resigned from the board of the OpenAI Foundation, two days after he announced he was stepping away from public commitments due to new revelations about his interactions with Jeffrey Epstein. 

“In line with my announcement to step away from my public commitments, I have also decided to resign from the board of OpenAI,” Summers said in a statement, according to Politico. “I am grateful for the opportunity to have served, excited about the potential of the company, and look forward to following their progress.”

The OpenAI Foundation, a nonprofit organization, controls and holds a roughly $130 billion stake in the for-profit OpenAI Group, according to its website. Microsoft (MSFT) also holds a major stake in the for-profit entity, which is best known for its AI chatbot ChatGPT. 

  • Warner Music Group (WMG) and AI music platform Udio have signed a licensing agreement to create a "next-generation AI music service."

"This arrangement additionally resolves the companies’ copyright litigation. Udio’s reimagined platform will introduce a suite of creative experiences that enable users to make remixes, covers, and new songs using the voices and styles of artists who choose to participate," Udio said in a statement. 

"Crucially, the collaboration will generate new revenue streams for artists and songwriters, while providing safeguards and usage guardrails for participating musicians," Udio added.

Udio announced a similar partnership with Universal Music Group (OTCPK:UMGNF) (OTCPK:UNVGY) in October. 

Warner, Universal, and Sony (SONY) are the music industry's three largest recording companies.  

  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Cisco (CSCO) and Saudi Arabian AI developer Humain are launching a joint venture focused on building data centers in the Middle East.

The JV's first project will be a 100-megawatt data center in Saudi Arabia to supply AI video startup Luma AI, Humain CEO Tareq Amin told Reuters. 

The name of the JV has not yet been announced, Reuters added. Humain is backed by Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund.

  • Luma AI, meanwhile, announced it has raised $900 million through a new funding round.

The round was led by Humain. Participants included AMD's (AMD) venture arm, Andreessen Horowitz, Amplify Partners and Matrix Partners. Luma is now valued at over $4 billion, according to CNBC.
